istanbul.name: Princes' Islands
istanbul.summary: Car-free islands in the Sea of Marmara — Büyükada is the largest. Bicycle the perimeter, eat lunch at a fish restaurant, ferry back at sunset.

istanbul.name: Bosphorus full cruise (Anadolu Kavağı)
istanbul.summary: Public IDO ferry from Eminönü all the way to Anadolu Kavağı near the Black Sea mouth. Hike to Yoros Castle, eat fish at the dock, return ferry.

istanbul.name: Bursa
istanbul.summary: First Ottoman capital. Mosque complexes, original silk-trade caravanserais, the green-tiled Yeşil Türbe. Best İskender kebab in the country.

istanbul.name: Şile + Ağva (Black Sea villages)
istanbul.summary: Black Sea fishing villages weekenders go to escape Istanbul. Beaches, river-mouth restaurants, lighthouse hikes.

istanbul.name: Polonezköy
istanbul.summary: Polish-founded village from 1842, now a forest brunch destination on the Asian side. Hiking trails, traditional Polish-Turkish food at Adampol.

istanbul.name: Gallipoli battlefield (Çanakkale)
istanbul.summary: WWI Anzac/Turkish memorial sites, museum, the Trojan Horse at Çanakkale. Heavy historical day, especially around April 25 (Anzac Day).

cappadocia.name: Ihlara Valley + Selime Monastery
cappadocia.summary: The Green Tour route. 14 km canyon hike past Byzantine cave churches, then the Star Wars-filming-location Selime Monastery cut into a cliff.

cappadocia.name: Soğanlı Valley
cappadocia.summary: Less-visited valley with painted Byzantine churches, dovecote-pocked tuff cliffs, and a dwindling village famous for cloth dolls.

cappadocia.name: Derinkuyu + Kaymaklı underground cities
cappadocia.summary: Two of the largest underground cities, 8 levels deep at Derinkuyu. Sheltered 20,000+ people in the Christian-Roman era. Claustrophobics, prepare.

cappadocia.name: Mount Hasan + Aksaray
cappadocia.summary: The 3,253m volcano whose ash sculpted Cappadocia. Seljuk-era caravanserais along the silk road, Sultanhanı caravanserai is the photo.

cappadocia.name: Hot-air balloon flight
cappadocia.summary: Not technically a day trip but the main reason people come. Pickup 4:30am, 60–90 minutes flight, back by 8am for breakfast. See our complete balloon guide.

antalya.name: Aspendos + Side ruins
antalya.summary: Aspendos is the best-preserved Roman amphitheater in the Mediterranean and still hosts opera in summer. Side adds beach + Apollo Temple ruins by sunset.

antalya.name: Termessos
antalya.summary: Roman-Pisidian mountain ruins Alexander couldn't conquer, now overgrown with pine forest. Steep hike to the necropolis. Bring water and good shoes.

antalya.name: Köprülü Canyon + rafting
antalya.summary: Canyon rafting + Selge Roman ruins. Pick rafting if you have kids; pick hiking + lunch by the river if you don't. Operator pickups daily.

antalya.name: Pamukkale + Hierapolis
antalya.summary: The white travertine pools and the Roman city above. Best as overnight to catch sunrise on the terraces, but workable as day trip if you start at 6am.

antalya.name: Phaselis ruins + beach
antalya.summary: Roman ruins on a triple-cove beach — the only place in Turkey you can swim while looking at a Roman aqueduct. Family-friendly.

izmir.name: Ephesus + Selçuk + Şirince
izmir.summary: The Mediterranean's most complete Roman city, the basilica of St. John, and a wine-producing hilltop village for lunch. The classic combo.

izmir.name: Pergamon (Bergama)
izmir.summary: Greek-Roman acropolis on a 300m hill, the Asclepion healing center, the world's steepest theater. Quieter than Ephesus.

izmir.name: Çeşme + Alaçatı
izmir.summary: Hipster wind-surfer's coast. Çeşme for thermal pools and seafood, Alaçatı for cobbled streets and stone-house boutique cafes.

izmir.name: Sardis + Mount Tmolus
izmir.summary: Lydian-era ruins (the kingdom that invented coins) plus Bin Tepe burial mounds. Less touristy than Ephesus, more interesting if you've seen Ephesus.

bodrum.name: Pamukkale + Hierapolis
bodrum.summary: Possible from Bodrum but you spend more time in the bus than on the terraces. Better as overnight; if doing as day trip, leave 5am.

bodrum.name: Ephesus from Bodrum
bodrum.summary: Yes you can. But you'll be in transit 6+ hours. Worth it once if you don't get to Izmir/Kuşadası on the same trip.

bodrum.name: Datça peninsula
bodrum.summary: Quieter, narrower peninsula west of Marmaris. Knidos ancient ruins at the very tip, where Aegean meets Mediterranean. Lots of swimming.

bodrum.name: Greek island day trip (Kos)
bodrum.summary: Daily ferries Bodrum → Kos. Schengen visa NOT required for day trips with passport. Bring it. Asklepion ruins, sandy beaches, lunch in Kos town.

fethiye.name: Saklıkent Gorge
fethiye.summary: Wade through ice-cold canyon water deep into a slot. Full day adds Tlos ruins and a trout lunch on platforms over the river.

fethiye.name: Kayaköy ghost village
fethiye.summary: Greek village abandoned 1923 in the population exchange. Eerie at sunset. Walk back to Ölüdeniz over the hill.

fethiye.name: Butterfly Valley
fethiye.summary: Cliff-protected cove only reachable by boat (or a vertical hike with ropes). Tiger butterflies, swim, picnic. Day-boat trips run from Ölüdeniz.

fethiye.name: 12 Islands boat tour
fethiye.summary: Daily boats from Fethiye Marina circle the surrounding islands. Multiple swim stops, lunch on board. Touristy but good.

fethiye.name: Tlos + Yakaköy
fethiye.summary: Lycian rock tombs cut into a cliff face above an old Ottoman village now full of small wineries. The under-the-radar Lycian site.
